content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. Let's define a function f(l,r)f(l, r)f(l,r) in the following way:


If 1‚â§l‚â§r‚â§N1 \le l \le r \le N1‚â§l‚â§r‚â§N, then f(l,r)f(l, r)f(l,r) is the maximum of Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_rAl‚Äã,Al+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ar‚Äã.
Otherwise, f(l,r)f(l, r)f(l,r) is 000.
Chef wants to choose a subset SSS of the set of indices {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N} such that ‚à£S‚à£‚â•K|S| \ge K‚à£S‚à£‚â•K. Let's define C=‚àëi‚ààS‚àëj‚ààSf(i,j)C = \sum_{i \in S}\sum_{j \in S} f(i, j)C=‚àëi‚ààS‚Äã‚àëj‚ààS‚Äãf(i,j).

Can you help Chef find the smallest value of CCC he can get?
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the minimum value of CCC.
